userNote: Out-of-scope-for-st8 follow-up. The founder mentioned 'a couple errors but I've built it successfully before' about the standalone PyQt6 app. Louis/FIXES_APPLIED.md documents v1→v2 fixes (setup-wizard timing, path handling, layout-clearing on None, folder-checkboxes init order) but PyQt6 widget lifecycle bugs are notoriously easy to miss. Right places to audit if/when the standalone app gets attention again: LouisTab.__init__, ConnieTab.__init__, CarlTab.__init__ — race conditions on signal connect and uninitialized widget refs. Does NOT block the st8 port; Path C reimplements the Warden in Node, so any residual Python bugs do not migrate. Note: the Louis/ directory is not present in this working tree (uploaded by founder at commit 1df677b on master); this ticket is a future-self reminder, not an immediately actionable item.

userNote: Forward-looking — three new routes. POST /api/lock { path } → resolves filepath via file_registry, calls features/locks/lock-manager.lockFile(absPath), persists via features/locks/lock-state.setLocked(fingerprint, true), fires HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED. POST /api/unlock symmetric. GET /api/locks → returns features/locks/lock-state.listLocked(). All three share the same path-resolution helper. Mount the routes next to /api/record-commit and /api/tickets which follow the same hook-fire-after-write pattern.
actionsTaken: Forward-looking — defer to docs/_pending-roadmap/louis-and-locking.md Phase L1: The locking primitive + state + routes (specifically the 'Add three routes to src/core/server/app.js' bullet, lines 66-71). POST routes require X-St8-Secret per the established auth pattern; all three share a path→fingerprint resolution helper; POST handlers call lockFile/unlockFile then setLocked then fire HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED. Cross-cluster dependencies: depends on lock-manager.js (ticket 10), lock-state.js (ticket 11), HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED (ticket 5), and the locked column (ticket 4) — all within Phase L1.
residualConcerns: Last-to-land within Phase L1 — exit criterion 'curl -X POST /api/lock flips a file to read-only AND records in SQLite AND fires hook AND emits SSE' is gated on this ticket.

userNote: Forward-looking — bootstrap migration question. When the lock column first lands, what about files already chmod 444'd on disk (pre-existing read-only files, files Louis-standalone locked)? Bootstrap should SELECT filepath FROM file_registry, isWritable()-check each, and seed locked = 1 for paths the OS reports read-only. Without this step the first-run state is inconsistent — SQLite says unlocked, OS says locked, panel UI shows lies. Bundle with the column-add migration in Phase L1.
actionsTaken: Forward-looking — defer to docs/_pending-roadmap/louis-and-locking.md Phase L1: The locking primitive + state + routes (specifically the 'Bootstrap migration step' clause inside the file_registry column-add bullet, lines 42-46). Scope: Bundle with the locked-column migration in src/core/database/persistence.js — on first-run after the column lands, iterate every file_registry row and seed locked = 1 for paths the OS reports read-only (via lock-manager.isWritable() check). Avoids first-run state inconsistency where SQLite says unlocked but disk says read-only (pre-existing chmod 444 files, files Louis-standalone locked). Cross-cluster dependencies: depends on ticket 4 (column landing) + ticket 10 (lock-manager.isWritable available).
residualConcerns: Severity 'med' (only med-severity ticket in the cluster) because first-run lies in the panel UI would damage trust early. Must ship in the same commit as the column-add migration to keep state coherent.

userNote: Forward-looking — open question on lock-history audit trail. Louis writes ~/.louis-control/lock-history.log line-by-line. st8 already has file_mutation_log (with mutationType enum that INCLUDES 'LOCK' but never fires it — see bible line 1617's 'Defined-but-never-fired' note). Right merge: a LOCK_CHANGED default subscriber that INSERTs a row into file_mutation_log with mutationType='LOCK', actor=(future) 'DEVELOPER'|'AGENT'|'SYSTEM'. Removes the need for a separate lock-history.log inside ~/.louis-control. Decide before Phase L1 lands so the hook-firing path is right the first time.
wave8aOutcome: DECIDED: option (a) — st8 reuses existing audit surfaces; no separate lock-history.log file. Recorded in docs/components/louis-and-locking.md 'Known unknowns' section (the bullet titled 'Lock-history audit trail — DECIDED (Wave 8A, ticket 15)'). The decision binds two existing tables: (1) file_mutation_log gets a LOCK row per change via the P=20 lock-mutation-log subscriber (finally firing the long-declared LOCK enum value), (2) activity_log gets a parallel row via a P=30 lock-activity-log subscriber for the cross-cutting timeline. Both tables exist today at persistence.js:117 (file_mutation_log) and persistence.js:129 (activity_log) with the right schema (targetFingerprint, action, source, details, timestamp). The Phase L1 hook-firing path is now spec'd, no further open question. No source edit needed in default-subscribers.js this wave — the subscribers themselves are L1 work; this ticket only had to PICK the audit-surface strategy, which is now done.
wave8aReviewNote: Independently verified activity_log schema at persistence.js:129-136: columns id, timestamp, source, action, targetFingerprint, details — exactly the columns needed for lock-history capture (target+action+source+details+timestamp). file_mutation_log at persistence.js:117-127 has actor, mutationType, fingerprint, metadata — supports the long-declared LOCK enum. Decision (option a) is correct: no new persistence surface, queryable via SQL, travels with st8.sqlite, single source of truth. P=20 + P=30 subscriber chain spec'd; ready for Phase L1 implementation.
